item.title: Day 7/100- Conquering Challenges on LeetCode
item.description:
Sivaraghavi

Posted on Aug 22

 

Hello everyone,

Day 7 of this coding journey has been all about embracing challenges and putting my skills to the test on a new platform—LeetCode! 💻

Today, I decided to switch things up and dive into some problems on LeetCode. It's an exhilarating experience to tackle a variety of questions that push me to think critically and apply my coding knowledge.

Solving problems on platforms like LeetCode not only enhances problem-solving skills but also exposes us to different approaches and techniques used in the coding world.

As I wrap up today's update, I'm feeling accomplished and motivated to keep exploring new avenues within the coding realm. Remember, each challenge we overcome is a step closer to our coding goals.

Stay determined,
Sivaraghavi

Rob Kendal {{☕}} - Aug 22

Once suspended, sivaraghavi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, sivaraghavi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by sivaraghavi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If sivaraghavi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag sivaraghavi:

Make all posts by sivaraghavi less visible

sivaraghavi consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging sivaraghavi will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: sivaraghavi

item.title: Day 31: Async Await
item.description:
Dharan Ganesan

Posted on Aug 22

The introduction of async and await in ECMAScript 2017 (ES8) revolutionized how developers deal with asynchronous tasks, making code more readable and maintainable. Before async and await, developers relied on callbacks and promises to manage asynchronous operations. Callbacks led to the infamous "callback hell," while promises improved code readability. However, promises still required handling .then() and .catch() chains, which could get complex in more intricate scenarios.

🏄 Enter async/await

async and await are built on top of promises and offer a more elegant and intuitive way to work with asynchronous code. async is used to declare an asynchronous function, and within that function, you can use await to pause execution until a promise is resolved.

async function fetchData(url) { try { const response = await fetch(url); const data = await response.json(); return data; } catch (error) { console.error("Error fetching data:", error); throw error; }
} // Usage
fetchData("https://api.example.com/data") .then(result => console.log(result)) .catch(error => console.error(error));

Benefits of async/await

  1. Readability: Asynchronous code reads almost like synchronous code, making it easier for developers to understand the flow of execution.

  2. Error Handling: With try-catch blocks, error handling becomes more natural and localized within the function, improving debugging.

  3. Sequential Code: await allows you to write sequential code even when dealing with asynchronous tasks, enhancing the logical structure of your program.

  4. Promise Chaining Reduction: async/await eliminates long chains of .then() and .catch() calls, leading to cleaner code.

  5. Concurrent Asynchronous Calls: You can use Promise.all() with await to perform multiple asynchronous operations concurrently.

async function fetchMultipleData(urls) { const promises = urls.map(url => fetchData(url)); const results = await Promise.all(promises); return results;
} // Usage
fetchMultipleData([ "https://api.example.com/data/1", "https://api.example.com/data/2"
]).then(results => console.log(results));

⚠️ Potential Pitfalls and Best Practices

  1. Not Always Necessary: Not every function needs to be async. Only use it when you need to pause the function's execution to wait for a promise to resolve.

  2. Avoid Blocking: Although await can block the function, it doesn't block the whole thread, ensuring other tasks can still be executed.

  3. Unhandled Promise Rejections: Ensure you have appropriate error handling in place using try-catch or .catch() to prevent unhandled promise rejections.

  4. Sequential vs. Parallel: Be mindful of whether tasks should be executed sequentially or in parallel, and structure your code accordingly.

Christopher Howard - Aug 2

Once suspended, dhrn will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dhrn will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dhrn will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dhrn is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag dhrn:

Make all posts by dhrn less visible

dhrn consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dhrn will restore default visibility to their posts.
